data.text: Executing bead `ddx-689471d1` in scope: I’m checking the current implementation and existing readiness tests first so the fix stays within the decoder and prompt contract the bead names.

The decoder is strict exactly where the bead says it is: `waivers_applied` and child `acceptance` still unmarshal directly into structured slices. I’m pulling the existing readiness tests next so the change lands in the same test shape already used here.

I have the main test surface now. The cleanest fit is to normalize those two JSON fields in the decoder and add prompt assertions against `buildPreClaimIntakePrompt`, rather than widening behavior elsewhere.

data.text: I’m at the edit point now. The decoder change can stay local by giving those fields custom `UnmarshalJSON` implementations, and the prompt change just needs to make the preferred shapes explicit instead of relying on the struct names alone.

data.text: I’m editing `preclaim_intake_hook.go` to make the two readiness-only fields tolerant of the observed scalar/list variants, then I’ll add decoder assertions and prompt-shape assertions around that behavior.
The code changes are in place. I’m formatting the touched Go files and then running the bead’s targeted readiness tests before I do the required `pre-commit` gate.

data.text: The first pass exposed one detail: for scalar child acceptance, the useful normalization is newline-splitting into criteria lines, not wrapping the whole blob as a single entry. I’m tightening that now so the string and array forms converge to the same internal shape.

data.text: `pre-commit` returned clean but skipped checks because nothing was staged yet. I’m staging just the three touched files and rerunning the hook so the actual gated checks run against this change set.

The staged hook is still running through the real checks now, including build/test steps. I’m waiting on that to finish before I commit; if it trips anything, I’ll fix it in-place rather than leaving a red bead attempt.

The hook is taking the slow path through the repo-wide checks, but there’s no failure output so far. I’m continuing to wait rather than interrupting it, because this is the last gate before the single required commit.

data.text:
Implemented the bead in [preclaim_intake_hook.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-689471d1-20260515T104757-0ab57b74/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go): readiness decoding now tolerates `waivers_applied` as either a flat string list or an object list, and `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` as either a single string or a string array. For scalar child acceptance, the decoder normalizes newline-delimited criteria into the same internal list shape. The intake prompt now explicitly documents the preferred output shapes and notes that those scalar fallbacks are decoder-tolerated only.

Added coverage in [readiness_classification_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-689471d1-20260515T104757-0ab57b74/cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go) and [preclaim_intake_rewrite_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-689471d1-20260515T104757-0ab57b74/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite_test.go) for both waiver variants, both child-acceptance variants, the prompt-shape contract, and the existing fractional-score path.

Verification passed with `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreClaimReadiness_.*(Waiver|SuggestedChild|FractionalScore)|TestPreClaimReadinessPromptSpecifiesOutputShapes" -count=1` and `lefthook run pre-commit`. Commit: `205aa3eac` (`fix: normalize readiness model output variants [ddx-689471d1]`).
